items.title: Choosing a Textbook
items.script:
W: Professor Li, I can't find the textbook you recommended. Is there an alternative?
M: Yes, you can use the second edition instead. It covers the same chapters.
W: Great. Can I borrow it from the library?
M: There are three copies on reserve. You can check one out for two hours.

questions.question: What does the professor suggest the woman do?
options: Borrow a copy from the library

items.title: Campus Shuttle Schedule Change
items.script: Attention, students. Due to road construction near Gate 2, the evening campus shuttle will depart from Gate 4 starting Monday. The schedule remains the same. Last departure is at 10:30 p.m. Please allow extra walking time to reach the new stop.

questions.question: What has changed about the campus shuttle?

items.title: Job Interview Preparation
items.script:
M: I have a job interview tomorrow and I'm really nervous.
W: Have you researched the company?
M: A little, but I haven't prepared answers to common questions yet.
W: Start with 'Tell me about yourself.' That's almost always the first question.

questions.question: What does the woman advise the man to prepare first?

items.title: Lost Student ID
items.script:
W: Excuse me, I lost my student ID. How do I get a replacement?
M: You'll need to fill out a form at the student affairs office and pay a fee of ten yuan.
W: How long will it take?
M: Usually three working days. You can pick it up at window five.

questions.question: Where should the woman go to get a replacement ID?

items.title: Dormitory Maintenance Notice
items.script: This is an announcement for residents of Building C. Hot water will be unavailable from 8 a.m. to noon on Saturday for routine pipe maintenance. Cold water supply will not be affected. We apologize for the inconvenience and thank you for your patience.

questions.question: What will be unavailable Saturday morning in Building C?

items.title: Study Group Arrangement
items.script:
M: Are we still meeting at the library at six?
W: I have a lab session until seven. Can we move it to seven-thirty?
M: That works. Should we reserve a group room?
W: I already did — Room 204 on the second floor.

questions.question: What time will the study group meet?

items.title: Choosing a Major
items.script:
W: I'm torn between economics and computer science. Which do you think is better?
M: It depends on what you enjoy. Economics suits analytical thinkers; CS suits problem-solvers.
W: I love both. Is there a combined program?
M: Yes, the business computing track lets you take courses in both departments.

questions.question: What does the man suggest the woman consider?

items.title: Library Fine Notice
items.script: Dear library users, please be reminded that overdue books incur a fine of 0.2 yuan per day. You may renew online up to twice before the item must be returned. Renewals are not possible if another user has placed a hold on the book. Check your account before the due date.

questions.question: When is online renewal NOT possible?
options: When the fine exceeds ten yuan
When another user has reserved the book
After the book is one week overdue

items.title: Planning a Graduation Trip
items.script:
M: Where should we go for the graduation trip? Some people want the beach, others the mountains.
W: Why not vote? We could send a survey to the whole class.
M: Good idea. And we should book early — prices double in summer.
W: I'll set up the survey tonight and share it in the group chat.

questions.question: What will the woman do tonight?
options: Create a survey for the class

items.title: Health Center Announcement
items.script: The campus health center will offer free flu vaccinations next Thursday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. Students must bring their student ID. No appointment is needed. The clinic is located in Building A, Room 101. Quantities are limited, so early arrival is recommended.

questions.question: What do students need to bring for the vaccination?

items.title: Requesting a Grade Review
items.script:
W: I think there's a mistake in my exam grade. Who should I contact?
M: You need to submit a grade review form within five days of the result release.
W: Can I email the professor directly?
M: Only after you submit the official form through the academic affairs portal.

questions.question: What must the woman do before contacting the professor?

items.title: Part-time Job Search
items.script:
M: I'm looking for a part-time job near campus. Any suggestions?
W: The coffee shop on West Street is hiring. It's only a ten-minute walk.
M: What are the hours?
W: Weekends only, nine to five. The pay is sixteen yuan an hour.

questions.question: When does the coffee shop need workers?

items.title: Campus Event Cancelled
items.script: We regret to inform you that this Saturday's outdoor film screening has been cancelled due to the weather forecast. A replacement indoor screening will take place in Lecture Hall B at the same time — 7 p.m. Seating is limited to 200. Arrive early to secure a seat.

questions.question: Why was the outdoor screening cancelled?

items.title: Renting an Apartment
items.script:
W: I found a two-bedroom apartment near the university for 2,800 yuan a month.
M: That sounds reasonable. Is it furnished?
W: Yes, basic furniture is included, but there's no washing machine.
M: You could use the laundry room on the ground floor. Most buildings have one.

questions.question: What is NOT included in the apartment?

items.title: Sports Center Policy Update
items.script: The sports center will now require all users to register online at least one hour before their session. Walk-ins will no longer be accepted during peak hours — 6 to 9 p.m. on weekdays. Weekend slots remain open for walk-ins. Student ID must be scanned at entry.

questions.question: When must users register online in advance?
